sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 7.57 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.7)

statement.latex: a) A set of generators of a finitely generated group$G$consisting of the least possible number$d(G)$of elements is called a basis of$G$. Let$r_M(G)$be the least number of relations necessary to define$G$in the basis$M$and let$r(G)$be the minimum of the numbers$r_M(G)$over all bases$M$of$G$. It is known that$r_M(G) d(G) + r(G)$for any basis$M$. Does there exist a finitely presented group$G$for whic...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
